coreIssue: This performance conversation is likely about whether Tom is calibrating to Principal scope on the new team or still delivering below the level of visible leverage expected.
openingQuestion: What situations on this team have shown you where Principal judgment and cross-team alignment are expected from you, and where is that still unclear?
listenFor: whether he names a specific recent decision, stakeholder conversation, or project where he set expectations early
whether if asked about Principal scope, they reference concrete team signals, examples, or people rather than speaking in abstractions
whether he pauses, hedges, or redirects when the discussion turns to tradeoffs, escalation, or handling hard news across teams
avoid: do not turn this into a generic delivery review when the real question is Principal-level scope and influence
do not define the Principal bar for him too early before testing how he currently reads expectations and judgment calls
goodOutcome: You and Tom have agreed what Principal-level impact means on this team in one concrete area of cross-team ownership he should visibly lead.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one recent case where earlier expectation alignment or a visible judgment call would have changed the outcome, so you can test his read of Principal scope against something real.
